bag of choices presents itself when the author considers calculated risks bag of strategy a involves a forty percent chance of winning one thousand dollars bag of strategy b involves a sixty percent chance of winning eight hundred dollars bag of expected value for strategy a is calculated by multiplying the probabilities with their respective outcomes bag of expected value for strategy a is four hundred minus two hundred which equals two hundred bag of expected value for strategy b is four hundred and eighty minus one hundred twenty which equals three hundred sixty bag of comparison shows that strategy b yields a higher expected value bag of decision is clear the author should choose strategy b for better returns
